Tiruppur is India's knitwear export capital. Vehicle growth reflects its booming garment industry.
TNSTC city and intercity routes in Tiruppur connecting garment workers to industrial zones.
Tiruppur's textile dyeing industry has historically been a significant source of water and air pollution. Air quality monitoring shows elevated levels.
Tiruppur accounts for over 50% of India's knitwear exports. It supplies global brands and has a thriving MSME ecosystem.

Tiruvallur borders Chennai and hosts major industrial zones (Sriperumbudur, Ambattur). Vehicle growth is among TN's highest.
High bus connectivity driven by worker commutes to Chennai's suburban industrial zones.
Air quality in Tiruvallur is under pressure from its dense industrial zones. Pollution levels are rising with expansion.
Sriperumbudur-Oragadam in Tiruvallur is TN's largest industrial cluster, home to Samsung, Hyundai, and Dell factories.

Villupuram has one of the largest government school networks in TN, serving a large rural and tribal population.
